purpose: Developer-specific rules and style preferences. Override knowledge/ dictionaries. Edit freely.
instruction_to_cursor: These rules take precedence over knowledge/ dictionaries. When conflict exists, follow this file.

naming.database_ids: Always name primary keys 'id', never 'uuid' or '{table}_id' for the PK
naming.booleans: Prefix with is_ or has_ or can_ — never 'active', 'enabled', 'flag'
naming.async_functions: Never suffix with _async — async is visible from signature
naming.private_functions: Single underscore prefix only — never double underscore except dunder
naming.constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE in constants.py — never inline in function bodies

python_patterns.early_returns: Prefer early returns for guard clauses — never deeply nested if-else
python_patterns.list_comprehensions: Simple transforms only. Condition AND transform -> use a for loop.
python_patterns.string_formatting: f-strings for user-facing. %-formatting for log messages only.

function_design.single_responsibility: If you need 'and' to describe what it does, split it
function_design.max_parameters: No more than 4. Group into dataclass if more needed.
function_design.return_types: Always explicit. Never implicit None on functions doing real work.
function_design.side_effects: Functions returning values should not have side effects.

testing.one_assert: Preferred — each test proves one thing
testing.no_magic_numbers: All test values must be named constants or fixture parameters

comments.rule: Comment WHY, never WHAT. If code needs a comment to explain what it does, rewrite it.
comments.never: Commented-out code — delete it, git history exists

style.line_count: More lines with clear intent over fewer compressed lines.
style.abstractions: Only add class/abstraction when same pattern appears 3+ times.
style.variable_names: Full words over abbreviations except universal (i, n, e for exceptions)
style.function_length: Under 30 lines. Approach 30 -> look for natural extraction.

output_messages.tone: Direct and informative. Never cutesy. Minimal emoji.
output_messages.success: State what was done and where. e.g. 'Generated UC-2026-0001 → ./use-cases/...'
output_messages.error: State what failed, why if known, what to check.
output_messages.progress: Show what stage is running — users should never wonder if it hung.
